data.title: 'Tesla to Stop Selling FSD After Feb 14,' Says Elon Musk
data.description:
Electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla (TSLA) will stop selling its Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology after February 14, CEO Elon Musk said in an X post. The tech will only be available as a monthly subscription thereafter. He noted that there would be some changes to how customers can access the autonomous driving technology for their EVs. FSD (Supervised) is a supervised partial vehicle automation system.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Until now, customers could buy FSD for an upfront payment of $8,000 or subscribe for a monthly fee of $99. Musk did not disclose further details on future pricing. Musk has positioned self-driving tech and robotics as Tesla's key growth engines, especially as the EV maker faces two straight years of declining car sales.

Shift to Monthly Subscription Model for FSD

After its initial success and getting drivers hooked, Tesla has decided to pivot strategically to a monthly subscription model. The current U.S. subscription price to upgrade from Basic Autopilot or Enhanced Autopilot to FSD is $99 per month, with Enhanced Autopilot (EAP) not available in all markets.

FSD is currently available in the U.S., Canada, China, Mexico, Puerto Rico, Australia, and New Zealand, and is expected to be rolled out to other regions in future updates. The features include semi-autonomous navigation, response to traffic lights and stop signs, lane change assistance, and self-parking. Musk's post does not clarify what happens to people who already bought FSD outright, but existing purchases could probably remain active. The change mainly affects new buyers; subscription users likely will not be able to switch to a one-time purchase later.

Last year, the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) opened an investigation into 2.88 million Tesla vehicles with FSD, triggered by over 50 reports of traffic violations and crashes. Tesla now calls it "FSD (Supervised)" for passenger cars. Factories use an unsupervised version to move new cars from assembly to delivery lots.

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

On TipRanks, Tesla stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $401.93 implies 10.1% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained 12.8%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: NVDA vs. TSLA: Which Mag 7 Stock Offers Higher Upside, According to Analysts?
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) and Tesla (TSLA) are two of the most closely watched members of the "Magnificent Seven," but their paths into 2026 look very different. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we have compared NVDA and TSLA to see which stock offers higher upside to investors. Nvidia stock currently holds a Strong Buy rating, with an average price target of $264.97, implying an upside of 42.6% from current levels. On the other hand, TSLA stock carries a Hold consensus among 30 analysts. Tesla's average stock price target of $401.93 suggests a downside of 10%.

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Takes Key Step Toward Settling EEOC  Racism Lawsuit in California
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) and the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) have agreed to begin settlement negotiations to resolve a federal lawsuit accusing the automaker of allowing severe racial harassment at its Fremont, California factory. The move marks the most significant progress in the case since the EEOC first filed suit in 2023.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Court documents show both sides are working to select a mediator, with discussions expected to begin as early as March or April. If the talks fail, Tesla and the EEOC will submit proposals to the court by June 17, 2026, outlining how the lawsuit should proceed.

Key Allegations Placed by EEOC

The EEOC's lawsuit paints a troubling picture of workplace conditions for Black employees at Tesla's main factory. According to the complaint, workers were subjected to frequent racial slurs and retaliation against employees who reported harassment.

Also, racist graffiti, including swastikas and nooses, was found on desks, elevators, and even vehicles coming off the production line.

Further, the agency said the harassment dates back to at least 2015, and that Tesla failed to take adequate action despite repeated complaints.

Meanwhile, Tesla has denied wrongdoing, saying it was not aware of the alleged harassment or did not ignore complaints. The company has also accused the EEOC of "headline‑chasing" and using litigation for publicity.

The Fremont plant has faced several discrimination lawsuits over the years, including cases from California's civil rights agency and individual workers. In a recent ruling, a judge said more than 6,000 Black employees could not sue Tesla as a group, limiting the scope of the state case.

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the last three months. At $401.93, the average Tesla price target implies a 10.12% downside risk. The stock has gained 41.12% over the past six months.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: U.S.-China AI Clash: Nvidia H200 Exports Approved—With Strings Attached
data.description:
The U.S. and China are locked in a fierce race for AI dominance, with Nvidia (NVDA) at the center. President Donald Trump approved exports of advanced H200 AI chips to China, but with strict conditions. Meanwhile, Beijing is trying to cut reliance on American semiconductors and boost its domestic chip industry.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia has consistently emphasized China's importance as a key and potentially lucrative market. If tensions ease, Nvidia could gain access to a substantial Chinese market for its flagship H200 line, though conditions may impact margins and strategic positioning. The company's broader product roadmap depends on future generations and other lines that may or may not qualify for export.

U.S. Approves H200 Exports with Conditions

The Trump administration has implemented a framework allowing Nvidia's H200 chips to be sold to China under strict rules, including revenue-sharing or fees and limited share of shipments relative to domestic sales. This marks a shift from a near-term export ban to a controlled pathway for one of Nvidia's most advanced data-center chips.

Before shipping H200 chips to China, the U.S. Commerce Department requires third-party lab testing to verify AI capabilities. Nvidia must also certify adequate domestic U.S. supplies before any exports proceed, with reports indicating China cannot receive more than 50% of chips sold to American customers. These steps aim to balance market access with national security oversight.

China Approves Imports with Restrictions

After initial hesitation, China has also allowed imports of Nvidia's H200 AI chips but with stricter controls. Beijing has approved imports only for narrow purposes like university research and development, which underscores a cautious pivot away from broader market access despite U.S. export approvals.

This policy shift highlights Beijing's strategic balancing act between leveraging advanced foreign tech and bolstering domestic alternatives amid escalating U.S.-China tech rivalry. Regulators are also exploring mandates to pair any limited H200 purchases with buys of local Chinese AI chips, a move that could reshape Nvidia's competitive position by subsidizing homegrown innovation and reducing dependency on U.S. semiconductors.

Nvidia Sees Solid H200 Demand Despite Limits

Despite U.S.-China regulatory tensions, Nvidia reports robust demand for its H200 chips. Reuters previously reported that over two million units have been ordered at around $27,000 each, potentially generating $54 billion in revenue. Nvidia's H200 remains the most advanced chip legally available to many Chinese firms, as CEO Jensen Huang noted at CES 2026.

Is Nvidia a Good Share to Buy Now?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Nvidia's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $264.97 implies 42.6% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, NVDA shares have surged 41%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Coca-Cola Ditches Costa Coffee Sale — What It Signals for KO Investors
data.description:
Coca-Cola (KO) has reportedly scrapped plans to sell its Costa Coffee business after bids from private equity firms fell short of expectations. The decision suggests that the beverage giant sees greater long-term value in retaining and expanding the brand rather than selling at current valuations. For KO investors, the move signals that by walking away from low offers, Coca-Cola is demonstrating disciplined capital allocation, a stance generally positive for shareholder value. That said, Costa remains a relatively small part of Coca-Cola's overall business, making the near-term impact on KO stock limited.

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Slip After S&P 500 (SPX) Retreats From Record Levels
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged lower ahead of Wednesday's session after the S&P 500 (SPX) pulled back from record highs reached earlier in the week. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down a modest 0.11%, 0.27%, and 0.17%, respectively, at 12:06 a.m. EDT on January 14.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading session, all three major indexes closed in the red. The S&P 500 fell 0.2%, the Dow dropped nearly 400 points, and the Nasdaq Composite edged down 0.1%.

Financial stocks led the losses. Shares of JPMorgan Chase (JPM) fell more than 4% after its fourth-quarter investment banking fees appeared weaker than expected. Goldman Sachs (GS) and Bank of America (BAC) also declined.

On the inflation front, December's Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 2.7% from a year earlier. Investors will get another update on inflation with the release of the December Producer Price Index (PPI) before the market opens.

Earnings will also be in focus. Bank of America, Wells Fargo (WFC), and Citigroup (C) are all scheduled to report their quarterly results before the opening bell.

data.title: Salesforce (CRM) Turns Slackbot into a Powerful AI Assistant in Major Platform Upgrade
data.description:
Salesforce (CRM) has launched its redesigned Slackbot, turning the chat helper into a full‑fledged AI work assistant built directly inside Slack. The rollout marks a major step in the software giant's plan to make Slack the main hub for its enterprise AI tools, supported by its broader Agentforce 360 strategy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company said Slackbot has already been deployed internally and is the fastest-adopted product in the company's history, with some teams reporting time savings of up to 20 hours per week.

Companies like IBM (IBM), General Motors (GM), Amazon (AMZN), OpenAI, and Anthropic were among early testers during the preview phase.

Salesforce Turns Slackbot into an AI Work Assistant

Salesforce is giving Slack a major upgrade, with a new, smarter Slackbot that does much more than reminders. The new version acts as a personal AI agent, able to understand work context, find information from chats and files, and even handle actions like scheduling meetings or drafting documents, all without leaving Slack.

Among other key capabilities, the Slackbot can summarize threads, retrieve documents, and generate meeting notes. Also, it can pull customer data from Salesforce systems to prepare users for calls or account reviews.

Moreover, Slack executives say the bot performs real‑time retrieval rather than training on customer data, addressing concerns about privacy and data handling.

The update is now generally available for Business+ and Enterprise+ customers, with a phased rollout continuing through January and February.

Overall, the revamped Slackbot reflects Salesforce's goal of making Slack the main entry point for an AI-powered workplace where people and AI work together smoothly.

Is CRM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, CRM stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 31 Buys, 10 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $326.95, the average Salesforce stock price target implies a 35.63% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Cathie Wood Keeps Loading Up on This Biotech Stock — What's Driving the Steady Buying?
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) continued to buy shares of Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) on Tuesday, extending a recent buying trend in the stock. The firm's continued buying suggests it remains confident in the company's longer-term prospects, even as biotech stocks remain volatile.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In its latest trade update for January 13, 2026, ARK bought 40,067 shares of Intellia. Most of the shares were added through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), with a smaller portion purchased via the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG). The total value of the buy was about $458,000.

(Note: To see more of Cathie Wood's January 9 trades, click here — NTLA, PSNL, KMTUY, ILMN: Cathie Wood Buys Early Biotech Stocks, Cuts Stakes in Illumina.)

This was not a one-time move. A day earlier, ARK added 99,292 shares of Intellia, and earlier last week, it bought 216,411 shares. While Tuesday's trade was smaller, the steady buying shows ARK is still adding to the position.

The recent buying comes ahead of a potential catalyst. Intellia is set to present at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference on January 14, a forum where biotech companies often provide updates on their pipelines and plans. Investors will be watching closely for any updates on Intellia's CRISPR programs and next steps.

Is Intellia a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Intellia stock based on nine Buys, 12 Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months. At $16.50, the average NTLA stock price target implies a 40.55% upside potential. Year-to-date, shares of the company have gained 30.6%.

data.title: Strategy Stock (MSTR) Gains 6% after Rare Insider Buy
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) stock jumped more than 6% on Tuesday, with the move largely driven by a rare insider purchase, along with short-term relief around index eligibility and renewed attention on the company's Bitcoin holdings. The rebound comes after a steep selloff, with the stock down about 42% over the past three months.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Insider Steps In After Years of Selling

One of the biggest signals came from inside the company. Director Carl Rickertsen bought 5,000 shares of Strategy, according to a January 12 filing with the SEC. The purchase totaled about $779,000, with shares acquired at roughly $156 each.

In recent years, Rickertsen had only sold shares, making this his first open-market purchase since 2022.

MSCI Relief Offers Short-Term Support

The insider buying also comes just days after Strategy avoided a potential setback linked to MSCI. MSCI has been reviewing whether companies with more than 50% of their balance sheet tied to cryptocurrencies should remain eligible for its indices.

Given Strategy's heavy exposure to Bitcoin, the company was at risk. While MSCI has delayed any removal for now, the issue remains under review, offering short-term relief rather than a final resolution.

Strategy Adds to Its Bitcoin Holdings

Separately, Strategy added more Bitcoin to its balance sheet. The company said it bought 13,627 Bitcoin in one week, its largest purchase since July 2025. This brings Strategy's total Bitcoin holdings to nearly 687,410 coins, keeping it the world's largest corporate holder.

As a result, the stock continues to move closely with Bitcoin's price, which remains a key factor investors watch.

Is MSTR Stock a Buy Now?

On TipRanks, Strategy stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 12 Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Strategy price target of $453.17 implies an impressive 162% upside potential from current levels. Meanwhile, MSTR shares have lost 49% over the past year.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
